title: Build social media platform with posts, stories, reels, and subscriptions

requirements.text: Implement User data model in Motoko backend with fields: id (Principal), username (Text), email (Text), and subscription (Bool)

quotes: public type User = { id: Principal; username: Text; email: Text; subscription: Bool; };

acceptanceCriteria: User type is defined with all specified fields
Principal is used as unique identifier

requirements.text: Implement Post data model in Motoko backend with fields: id (Nat), author (Principal), content (Text), mediaUrl (Text), likes (array of Principal), and createdAt (Nat timestamp)

quotes: public type Post = { id: Nat; author: Principal; content: Text; mediaUrl: Text; likes: [Principal]; createdAt: Nat; };

acceptanceCriteria: Post type is defined with all specified fields
Likes are stored as array of Principal identifiers
Timestamp is stored as Nat

requirements.text: Create backend function createPost that accepts author (Principal), content (Text), and mediaUrl (Text), generates unique post ID, stores post in canister state, and returns the created Post

quotes: public func createPost(author: Principal, content: Text, mediaUrl: Text) : async Post

acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts author, content, and mediaUrl parameters
Auto-increments post ID
Returns newly created post object
Stores post in canister stable storage

requirements.text: Create backend function likePost that accepts postId (Nat) and user (Principal), adds user to post's likes array if not already present, and prevents duplicate likes from same user

quotes: Public functions: CreatePost, LikePost, GetFeed, SubscribeUser

acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts postId and user Principal
Adds user to likes array only if not already present
Returns updated post or success status

requirements.text: Create backend function getFeed that returns array of all posts sorted by creation time (newest first)

quotes: Public functions: CreatePost, LikePost, GetFeed, SubscribeUser

acceptanceCriteria: Returns array of Post objects
Posts are sorted by createdAt timestamp in descending order

requirements.text: Create backend function subscribeUser that accepts user Principal and sets their subscription status to true

quotes: Public functions: CreatePost, LikePost, GetFeed, SubscribeUser

acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts user Principal
Updates user subscription field to true
Returns success status or updated user object

requirements.text: Create frontend PostCard component that displays post content, media, author information, like count, and like button with state management
acceptanceCriteria: Component displays post content and media
Shows like count and author information
Like button calls backend likePost function
Uses React Query for optimistic updates

requirements.text: Create frontend Home page that fetches and displays feed of all posts using React Query, with infinite scroll or pagination
acceptanceCriteria: Page fetches posts using getFeed backend function
Posts are displayed in feed layout using PostCard components
Uses React Query for data fetching and caching
Responsive grid layout with Tailwind CSS

requirements.text: Create frontend Profile page that displays user information (username, email, subscription status) and user's posts
acceptanceCriteria: Page displays authenticated user information
Shows user's posts filtered by author
Displays subscription status badge
Responsive layout with Tailwind CSS

requirements.text: Create frontend Subscribe page with Stripe integration that initiates checkout session and updates user subscription status in backend upon successful payment
acceptanceCriteria: Page displays subscription plans and pricing
Stripe checkout button initiates payment flow
After successful payment, backend subscribeUser function is called
User is redirected back to app with success message

requirements.text: Create frontend StoryViewer component with carousel navigation that displays stories with media content and auto-advance timer
acceptanceCriteria: Component displays stories in fullscreen carousel
Auto-advances to next story after set duration
Supports tap navigation (left/right)
Shows progress indicators for multiple stories

requirements.text: Create frontend ReelPlayer component using HTML5 video tag that plays video content with controls, loop functionality, and like/comment interface overlay
acceptanceCriteria: Component uses HTML5 video element
Includes play/pause controls
Shows like and comment buttons overlaid on video
Supports vertical scroll between reels

requirements.text: Create frontend Navbar component with navigation links to Home, Profile, and Subscribe pages, plus authentication status indicator
acceptanceCriteria: Component displays navigation links
Shows Internet Identity login/logout button
Displays user avatar or username when authenticated
Responsive mobile menu for small screens

requirements.text: Implement backend data model for Stories with fields: id (Nat), author (Principal), mediaUrl (Text), expiresAt (Nat timestamp), and views (array of Principal)
acceptanceCriteria: Story type is defined with all specified fields
Includes expiration timestamp for 24-hour stories
Tracks viewers as array of Principals

requirements.text: Implement backend data model for Comments with fields: id (Nat), postId (Nat), author (Principal), content (Text), and createdAt (Nat timestamp)
acceptanceCriteria: Comment type is defined with all specified fields
Links to parent post via postId
Includes timestamp for sorting

requirements.text: Create backend function addComment that accepts postId (Nat), author (Principal), and content (Text), stores comment, and returns created Comment object
acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts postId, author, and content
Auto-generates comment ID
Stores comment in canister state
Returns created comment object

requirements.text: Create backend function getComments that accepts postId (Nat) and returns array of all comments for that post sorted by creation time
acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts postId parameter
Returns array of Comment objects
Comments are sorted by createdAt timestamp

requirements.text: Implement notification tracking in backend with data model containing: id (Nat), recipientId (Principal), type (Text for like/comment/follow), relatedId (Nat), senderId (Principal), read (Bool), and createdAt (Nat timestamp)

quotes: User likes → backend updates likes array → frontend polls or subscribes to update

acceptanceCriteria: Notification type is defined with all specified fields
Supports different notification types (like, comment, follow)
Tracks read/unread status

requirements.text: Create backend function getNotifications that accepts userId (Principal) and returns array of user's notifications sorted by creation time (newest first)
acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts userId parameter
Returns array of Notification objects for that user
Notifications are sorted by createdAt in descending order

requirements.text: Design a cohesive visual theme with warm, energetic colors (oranges, yellows, corals), modern typography with rounded sans-serif fonts, and card-based layouts with subtle shadows and smooth transitions throughout the application
acceptanceCriteria: Consistent color palette applied across all pages
Typography hierarchy is clear and readable
Interactive elements have smooth hover and transition effects
Card components have consistent styling with shadows and rounded corners

constraints: Use single-actor architecture with all Motoko logic in backend/main.mo
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: useInternetIdentity hooks, useActor.ts, main.tsx, or components/ui directory
Authentication must use Internet Identity only
No external databases - all data stored in IC canisters
No real-time WebSockets - use polling for notifications
Stripe integration requires external webhook handling
nonGoals: Music overlay or audio integration for stories
